rulings.Case Title: MIQ LOGISTICS, LLC VS. SUNLINK CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or In The Alternative, Correct Or Vacate Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 20, 2014, Line 2, RESPONDENT SUNLINK CORPORATION's Motion To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, Correct Or Vacate Arbitration Award. Denied. First, the motion is untimely. A response to a petition to confirm an arbitration award "shall be served and filed within 10 days after service of the petition." (CCP sec. 1290.6.) This includes a motion to vacate an arbitration award filed in response to a petition. (Oaktree Capital Management, L.P. v. Bernard (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 60, 66.) The arbitration award was dated March 16, 2014. The petition to confirm arbitration was personally served on Respondent SunLink Corporation ("Respondent") on April 1, 2014. Respondent did not file this motion until April 30, 2014. Even if the Court were to reach the merits, the motion is deniedhe evidence does not establish that the award was procured by fraud, as required by CCP section 1286.4. Respondent entered into a Joint Stipulation which formed the basis for the arbitration away in which it stipulated, among other matters, that it owed the principal amount of the invoices, plus interest; and reasonable attorney's fees and costs. (Petitioner MIQ Logistics, LLC Opposition to Motion to Dismiss, Ex. 1 ("Joint Stipulation").) Further, the arbitrator denied Respondent's requests for consolidation after considering the issue twice. "[T]he merits of a controversy that has been submitted to arbitration are not subject to judicial review." (Harris v. Sandro (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1310, 1313.) In any event, Respondent does not establish prejudice because there is another pending arbitration at which the remaining matters concerning the Prepanelization Agreement will be resolved. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: USAA CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY VS. ARTURO PENA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton And Motion For Leave To File A X-Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 20, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANTs ARTURO PENA and SUNSET SCAVENGER COMPANY's (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS RECOLOGY SUNSET SCAVENGER COMPANY) Motion For Leave To File A Cross Complaint. Granted. (CCP sec. 428.10.) Defendants may file their proposed cross-complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. Defendants' objection to evidence is sustained as lacking authentication. (Evid. Code sec. 1400.) Defendants have established that the allegations in the cross-complaint arise out of the same transaction or occurrence as the complaint. It serves the interests of justice to have all issues relating to the same car accident resolved at once in order to avoid multiplicity of actions, promote judicial economy, and prevent the risk of inconsistent verdicts. Defendants have not shown prejudice by the filing of the cross-complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No tentative. Parties to appear. =(302/JPT)
